paragraphs.summary: The Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) is required to publish technical information (TI) that it considers appropriate relating to the calculation of the technical provisions.1 This Statement of Po
This SoP is relevant to all UK Solvency II firms, including the Society of Lloyd’s and its managing agents, hereafter referred to as ‘UK insurers'.
The PRA refers to the currencies in which it publishes TI as ‘PRA relevant currencies’.
The PRA has adopted methodologies and judgements consistent with those the European Insurance and Occupational Pensions Authority (EIOPA) incorporates in its TI as at the end of the transition period

In determining the published technical information on the risk-free interest rate term structures for PRA relevant currencies the PRA uses techniques, data specifications, and parameters which are tra
The PRA’s approach to the calculation of the basic risk-free interest rate term structures for PRA relevant currencies is based on the following assumptions:
The PRA determines and publishes a volatility adjustment for each relevant currency. Those volatility adjustments:
The PRA’s TI is published on the Bank of England’s website.3

The PRA’s choice of PRA relevant currencies is based on the relative materiality of technical provisions denominated in each currency, and currencies in which UK insurers have non-zero technical provi
[DELETED]
The PRA chooses PRA relevant currencies using two criteria:

The PRA assesses materiality by selecting PRA relevant currencies to ensure that at least 99% of group technical provisions are covered which includes the Society of Lloyd’s’ solo technical provisions

When assessing the above materiality criterion, the PRA will use data from the calendar year-end returns of insurers. The returns data as at year-end N (or, if unavailable at that effective date, at t
The PRA used the above criteria to generate the initial list of PRA relevant currencies applicable from 31 December 2020. In subsequent years, the PRA may add currencies that meet either of the above
A currency may also be removed from the list of PRA relevant currencies, where:
The PRA aims to give firms at least three months’ notice of any addition or removal of a currency from the list of PRA relevant currencies.

Where a UK insurer has technical provisions in a particular currency for which the PRA does not publish TI, it is the firm’s responsibility to propose TI that complies with the requirements in the PRA

The PRA needs to make judgements about the reference instruments and associated Credit Risk Adjustments (CRAs) to use when constructing the basic RFRs for PRA relevant currencies. For example, some re

For the LTAS calculation, the PRA calculates the average of spreads over the RFR applicable at the time of the spread data. Therefore, following the transition of TI references from Libor to an OIS ra
The PRA focuses its assessment of the depth, liquidity, and transparency of the market (the DLT assessment) on interest rate swaps data for the markets of relevant currencies where there is an active

The PRA uses the same volume indicators as applied by EIOPA (at the end of the TP) for assessing the liquidity of the swaps market, but with the average (over one year) daily notional turnover require

The PRA also considers in its DLT assessment additional criteria developed to avoid excessive volatility in the results of the assessment, specifically that a previously liquid market needs to drop at

The PRA may also consider other metrics and expert opinion in order to supplement its assessment as required for the relevant currencies, which are set out in its published results and analysis of the

For non-GBP currencies, the PRA makes use of externally published DLT data and analysis, including that published by EIOPA and the International Association of Insurance Supervisors (IAIS), to contrib
[DELETED]
[DELETED]

The RP for a relevant currency is one that is representative of the assets which are denominated in that currency and which firms are invested in to cover the best estimate for insurance and reinsuran

The PRA derives VA RPs using a similar technical approach as EIOPA.5 Further details on this, including specific areas where the PRA’s approach differs, are explained below. The methods applied by the
For each relevant currency, the assets of a RP are those for which a reliable market value is observable, are valued in accordance with the rules in the Valuation Part of the P
The derivation of the VA RPs reflects UK firms’ asset exposures. Specifically:

The PRA applies a simplified approach to determining the weights for the government and corporate bond portfolios when ‘looking through’ into the underlying assets held within collective investment un
The PRA does not publish separate country VA RPs for any of the relevant currencies.

The PRA’s approach will only treat exposures to central governments and central banks as government bonds, with all bonds issued by regional governments and local authorities (RGLAs) classified as cor
The PRA aims to give firms at least three months’ notice of the change in the VA RPs. The PRA’s VA RPs will become effective from the next 31 March following publication.

The PRA calculates the risk-corrected currency spread referred to in paragraph [2.1C(b)](/guidance/statements-of-policy/sop---the-pras-approach-to-the-publication-of-solvency-ii-technical-information#

For each relevant currency the RP spread referred to in paragraph [3.13](/guidance/statements-of-policy/sop---the-pras-approach-to-the-publication-of-solvency-ii-technical-information#b4b766a6eb1e40ee

The PRA calculates the portion of the average currency spread that is attributable to a realistic assessment of expected losses, unexpected credit risk or any other risk referred to in paragraph [3.13

sections.chapter_title: Symmetric adjustment of the equity capital charge (SAECC)

paragraphs.summary: The PRA will calculate and publish an SAECC figure in line with the rules set out in the [Solvency Capital Requirement – Standard Formula Part](/pra-rules/solvency-capital-requirement---standard-formu
